result.summary: Use Defender-to-Intune security tasks when available, then configure the Windows endpoint setting through Intune Endpoint security or Settings Catalog. Area inferred from title: Windows endpoint configuration hardening.

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.
If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: block use of copied or impersonated system tools.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Block use of copied or impersonated system tools

steps: Open the matching recommendation in Microsoft Defender > Exposure management/Vulnerability management/Secure Score and review affected assets and Microsoft-provided remediation details.
Create a remediation request/activity if Defender supports it for this recommendation, or document why it is being handled through another control plane.
Track remediation status and avoid marking the work complete until Defender telemetry shows the recommendation has improved or been resolved.
defender.notes: Use Defender to review the recommendation, affected devices/entities, remediation options, exceptions, and closure tracking.

validation_steps: Verify the setting has been applied in the relevant portal
Check Defender Secure Score for improvement after 24 hours
Review device compliance status in Intune if applicable
risks: Test in a pilot group before broad deployment
Verify compatibility with existing policies before applying

references.title: Block use of copied or impersonated system tools

rollback.intune: Remove the configuration policy or set to Not Configured in Intune
rollback.gpo: Set the policy to Not Configured in Group Policy Management

result.summary: Not applicable as the primary implementation plane: Defender for Identity recommendations generally target Active Directory, AD CS, AD FS, Entra Connect, privileged accounts, or domain controllers.

steps: Open the assessment in Microsoft Secure Score/Defender for Identity and download or review the affected entities and remediation guidance.
For GPO-related findings, identify the exact GPO, setting, permission, or OU from the Defender assessment before editing.
Apply changes in a pilot or change-controlled GPO; validate effective policy and confirm Defender for Identity assessment status after it refreshes.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Identify Entra ID privileged accounts that are also privileged in Active Directory

steps: Open the Defender for Identity assessment and identify whether the affected identity/control is Entra ID, Entra Connect, cloud privileged roles, or on-premises AD.

For Entra-side remediation, use the matching Entra control such as role assignment, risk remediation, or hybrid sync configuration; do not change Entra settings unless the assessment identifies that plane.
Validate the cloud-side change in Entra audit/sign-in/risk logs and confirm the Defender assessment refreshes.
entra.notes: Conditional: this Defender for Identity finding touches hybrid/cloud identity; use Entra only for the cloud-side or hybrid identity control identified in the assessment.

steps: Open Microsoft Defender portal > Secure Score/Recommended actions and filter for the matching Defender for Identity recommendation.
Review the impacted entities, category, context, and remediation guidance supplied by the assessment.
Implement the change in the correct underlying system: AD, AD CS, AD FS, Entra Connect, domain controllers, or Entra ID, depending on the assessment details.
Recheck the assessment after implementation; Defender for Identity updates entities quickly, while score/status updates can take longer.
defender.notes: Use Defender for Identity security posture assessments in Secure Score to obtain affected entities, context, remediation paths, and validation. Area inferred from title: Active Directory account / privilege posture.

validation_steps: Verify the setting has been applied in the relevant portal
Check Defender Secure Score for improvement after 24 hours
Review device compliance status in Intune if applicable
risks: Test in a pilot group before broad deployment
Verify compatibility with existing policies before applying

references.title: Identify Entra ID privileged accounts that are also privileged in Active Directory

If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: set Minimum PIN length for startup = 6 or more characters.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.

If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: review Update Microsoft Defender for Endpoint core components.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Update Microsoft Defender for Endpoint core components

steps: Open the matching recommendation in Microsoft Defender > Exposure management/Vulnerability management/Secure Score and review affected assets and Microsoft-provided remediation details.
Create a remediation request/activity if Defender supports it for this recommendation, or document why it is being handled through another control plane.
Track remediation status and avoid marking the work complete until Defender telemetry shows the recommendation has improved or been resolved.
defender.notes: Use Defender to review the recommendation, affected devices/entities, remediation options, exceptions, and closure tracking.

validation_steps: Verify the setting has been applied in the relevant portal
Check Defender Secure Score for improvement after 24 hours
Review device compliance status in Intune if applicable
risks: Test in a pilot group before broad deployment
Verify compatibility with existing policies before applying

references.title: Update Microsoft Defender for Endpoint core components

Monitor reports/quarantine/policy results and confirm the Secure Score recommendation status updates.

# Build a detection script that returns noncompliant only when the current device state does not match the target from the workbook title.
# Build a remediation script that changes only the confirmed setting or service; do not script unverified registry paths, unsupported commands, or broad hardening changes.
# Upload the detection/remediation package in Intune Remediations, assign to a pilot group, and monitor detection/remediation results.
scripts.validation:
# Validation script
# Verify the setting was applied correctly

rollback.intune: Remove the configuration policy or set to Not Configured in Intune
rollback.gpo: Set the policy to Not Configured in Group Policy Management

result.summary: Use Defender-to-Intune security tasks when available, then configure the Windows endpoint setting through Intune Endpoint security or Settings Catalog. Area inferred from title: Remote access hardening.

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.
If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: disable 'Allow Basic authentication' for WinRM Service.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Disable 'Allow Basic authentication' for WinRM Service

steps: Open the matching recommendation in Microsoft Defender > Exposure management/Vulnerability management/Secure Score and review affected assets and Microsoft-provided remediation details.
Create a remediation request/activity if Defender supports it for this recommendation, or document why it is being handled through another control plane.
Track remediation status and avoid marking the work complete until Defender telemetry shows the recommendation has improved or been resolved.
defender.notes: Use Defender to review the recommendation, affected devices/entities, remediation options, exceptions, and closure tracking.

validation_steps: Verify the setting has been applied in the relevant portal
Check Defender Secure Score for improvement after 24 hours
Review device compliance status in Intune if applicable
risks: Test in a pilot group before broad deployment
Verify compatibility with existing policies before applying

references.title: Disable 'Allow Basic authentication' for WinRM Service

result.summary: Use Defender-to-Intune security tasks when available, then configure the Windows endpoint setting through Intune Endpoint security or Settings Catalog. Area inferred from title: Windows endpoint configuration hardening.

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.
If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: set IPv6 source routing = highest protection.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Set IPv6 source routing to highest protection

result.summary: Use Defender-to-Intune security tasks when available, then configure the Windows endpoint setting through Intune Endpoint security or Settings Catalog. Area inferred from title: Microsoft Defender Attack Surface Reduction (ASR).

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.
If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: block abuse of exploited vulnerable signed drivers.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.
If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: enable Microsoft Defender Antivirus email scanning.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Enable Microsoft Defender Antivirus email scanning

steps: Open the matching recommendation in Microsoft Defender > Exposure management/Vulnerability management/Secure Score and review affected assets and Microsoft-provided remediation details.
Create a remediation request/activity if Defender supports it for this recommendation, or document why it is being handled through another control plane.
Track remediation status and avoid marking the work complete until Defender telemetry shows the recommendation has improved or been resolved.
defender.notes: Use Defender to review the recommendation, affected devices/entities, remediation options, exceptions, and closure tracking.

validation_steps: Verify the setting has been applied in the relevant portal
Check Defender Secure Score for improvement after 24 hours
Review device compliance status in Intune if applicable
risks: Test in a pilot group before broad deployment
Verify compatibility with existing policies before applying

references.title: Enable Microsoft Defender Antivirus email scanning

# Verify the setting was applied correctly

rollback.intune: Remove the configuration policy or set to Not Configured in Intune
rollback.gpo: Set the policy to Not Configured in Group Policy Management

result.summary: Use Defender-to-Intune security tasks when available, then configure the Windows endpoint setting through Intune Endpoint security or Settings Catalog. Area inferred from title: Microsoft Defender Antivirus.

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.
If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: enable cloud-delivered protection.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Enable cloud-delivered protection

steps: Open the matching recommendation in Microsoft Defender > Exposure management/Vulnerability management/Secure Score and review affected assets and Microsoft-provided remediation details.
Create a remediation request/activity if Defender supports it for this recommendation, or document why it is being handled through another control plane.
Track remediation status and avoid marking the work complete until Defender telemetry shows the recommendation has improved or been resolved.
defender.notes: Use Defender to review the recommendation, affected devices/entities, remediation options, exceptions, and closure tracking.

validation_steps: Verify the setting has been applied in the relevant portal
Check Defender Secure Score for improvement after 24 hours
Review device compliance status in Intune if applicable
risks: Test in a pilot group before broad deployment
Verify compatibility with existing policies before applying

rollback.intune: Remove the configuration policy or set to Not Configured in Intune
rollback.gpo: Set the policy to Not Configured in Group Policy Management

result.summary: Use Defender-to-Intune security tasks when available, then configure the Windows endpoint setting through Intune Endpoint security or Settings Catalog. Area inferred from title: Windows endpoint configuration hardening.

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.
If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: enable Automatic Updates.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Enable Automatic Updates

steps: Open the matching recommendation in Microsoft Defender > Exposure management/Vulnerability management/Secure Score and review affected assets and Microsoft-provided remediation details.
Create a remediation request/activity if Defender supports it for this recommendation, or document why it is being handled through another control plane.
Track remediation status and avoid marking the work complete until Defender telemetry shows the recommendation has improved or been resolved.

result.summary: Use Defender-to-Intune security tasks when available, then configure the Windows endpoint setting through Intune Endpoint security or Settings Catalog. Area inferred from title: Browser or document application policy.

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.
If Defender offers a remediation request, submit it. In Intune, go to Endpoint security > Security tasks, accept the task, and follow the Defender-provided remediation details.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: disable Flash on Adobe Reader DC.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.
gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Disable Flash on Adobe Reader DC

steps: Open the matching recommendation in Microsoft Defender > Exposure management/Vulnerability management/Secure Score and review affected assets and Microsoft-provided remediation details.
Create a remediation request/activity if Defender supports it for this recommendation, or document why it is being handled through another control plane.
Track remediation status and avoid marking the work complete until Defender telemetry shows the recommendation has improved or been resolved.
defender.notes: Use Defender to review the recommendation, affected devices/entities, remediation options, exceptions, and closure tracking.

validation_steps: Verify the setting has been applied in the relevant portal
Check Defender Secure Score for improvement after 24 hours
Review device compliance status in Intune if applicable
risks: Test in a pilot group before broad deployment
Verify compatibility with existing policies before applying

references.title: Disable Flash on Adobe Reader DC

steps: In the Microsoft Defender portal, open the matching security recommendation and record affected devices, remediation type, and Microsoft's tenant-specific remediation text.

Create or update an Intune policy using Endpoint security profiles or Settings Catalog for the setting named in the workbook recommendation. Target state from workbook title: disable Microsoft Defender Firewall notifications when programs are blocked for Private profile.
Assign to a pilot device group, monitor Intune deployment/per-setting status, then expand the assignment only after validation.
Return to the Defender recommendation and verify the exposed device count/status updates after configuration telemetry is refreshed.
Confirm the setting in the workbook title exists in Windows security policy, Administrative Templates, or a documented Defender policy path for the target OS.
Create a pilot GPO or edit an existing hardening GPO in Group Policy Management; configure the target state from the workbook recommendation title.
Link the GPO to a pilot OU, refresh policy on test devices, and verify that the setting applies before broad rollout.
Confirm the matching Defender recommendation status and affected device count after telemetry refresh.

result.summary: Not applicable as the primary implementation plane: this is an email/collaboration service policy, not an endpoint configuration setting.

gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Set a daily message limit

steps: Use least-privileged Entra/Microsoft 365 admin roles for the administrator making the change.
Implement the actual recommendation in the service portal or PowerShell surface identified in the source-backed service implementation guide.
entra.notes: Conditional: Entra may be relevant for admin roles, Conditional Access, or app governance, but the recommendation itself is implemented in Defender for Office, Exchange Online, or Microsoft 365/Purview service settings.

steps: Open the Microsoft Defender portal and navigate to Email & collaboration > Policies & rules > Threat policies.
Open or create the policy area matching this recommendation (Outbound spam / mail forwarding) and configure the control to the workbook target title: Set a daily message limit.
Apply to a pilot or scoped recipient group first when possible, review precedence/conflicts with preset security policies, then broaden scope.
Monitor reports/quarantine/policy results and confirm the Secure Score recommendation status updates.
defender.notes: Implement in the Microsoft Defender portal under Email & collaboration policies. Area inferred from title: Outbound spam / mail forwarding.

validation_steps: Verify the setting has been applied in the relevant portal
Check Defender Secure Score for improvement after 24 hours
Review device compliance status in Intune if applicable
risks: Test in a pilot group before broad deployment
Verify compatibility with existing policies before applying

gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Ensure 'Self service password reset enabled' is set to 'All'

steps: In Microsoft Entra admin center, open Password reset settings.
Set self-service password reset to All to match the workbook recommendation.
Configure authentication methods/registration settings and test password reset with pilot users before broad communication.
entra.notes: Implement in Microsoft Entra ID. Area inferred from title: Self-service password reset.

After implementation in Entra, return to Secure Score to confirm the recommendation status/score updates.

gpo.policy_path: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings
gpo.setting_name: Designate more than one global admin

steps: Inventory current Entra and Microsoft 365 administrative role assignments.
Replace broad Global Administrator assignments with least-privileged roles where possible.
Ensure more than one appropriate emergency/admin coverage account exists while avoiding unnecessary permanent Global Administrator assignments.
Document role owners, review assignments regularly, and validate with Entra audit logs.
entra.notes: Implement in Microsoft Entra ID. Area inferred from title: Administrative role governance.

steps: Open Secure Score recommended actions and select the matching Entra recommendation.
